Your personal utility belt

Self-contained browser tools. No internet required. Download this file and open it anywhere.

🌐 ServiceNow Integration
Identity is auto-detected when the relay connects. If detection fails or finds the wrong account, search for yourself below.
▶ Advanced Options

🔑 Personal Access Token (PAT)

Generate a PAT in Jira → Profile → Personal Access Tokens to bypass the relay. Requires a local proxy server to avoid CORS restrictions. See CRG Settings for details.

🌐 PowerShell Proxy / Edge Launcher

Power users can run a localhost proxy or launch Edge with --disable-web-security to eliminate the relay entirely. See CRG Settings → Advanced for full documentation.

🔍

What this does

Injects a floating panel into any web app and extracts every form field, table column, and interactive element — capturing the human-readable label alongside the backend field name (name, ng-model, formControlName, v-model, data-* and more). Output is structured JSON you can hand directly to an AI or developer.

💡 Works on Salesforce, ServiceNow, Dynamics, SAP Fiori, Angular, React, Vue apps and plain HTML forms.
🔖

Install Bookmarklet

Recommended

Drag this button to your bookmarks bar. Then click it on any web page to run the extractor.

🧰 Extract Elements

Drag ↑ to bookmarks bar  ·  Click on any page to run

⚠️ If dragging is blocked, right-click the button → Copy Link, then paste it manually into a new bookmark's URL field.
📋

How to use

1
Install the bookmarkletDrag the "🧰 Extract Elements" button above to your browser's bookmarks bar.
2
Navigate to your target appOpen the web application page you want to map (a record form, data entry screen, etc.).
3
Click the bookmarkletA floating panel will appear showing a summary. Click the bookmarklet again to dismiss it.
4
Download or copy the JSONClick ⬇ Download JSON or Copy JSON in the panel. Share the output when asking for integration or data mapping help.
Alternative: DevTools Console snippet

Open DevTools (F12), go to Console, paste the code below, and press Enter.

Loading…
JSON output format reference
PropertyTypeDescription
meta.urlstringFull URL of the extracted page
meta.titlestringPage title
meta.extractedAtISO dateTimestamp of extraction
forms[].fields[]arrayFields within each form
fields[].labelstringHuman-readable label
fields[].namestringHTML name — most common backend key
fields[].attributesobjectng-model, formcontrolname, v-model, data-* etc.
orphanFields[]arrayFields outside any <form> (common in SPAs)
tables[].columns[]arrayTable column headers with data attributes
View extractor source code

Full unminified source injected into target pages. Review before using on sensitive systems.

        🔧 Generator — 5-step workflow: fetch Jira issues by fix version → review → preview docs → set environments → submit. Pulls live ServiceNow and Jira data via the relay to pre-populate fields. The generated CR can be copied as formatted text or submitted directly to SNow.

        🔍 SNow Field Inspector — Browse and inspect any ServiceNow Change Request by CHG number. Shows all fields and values for a given environment (REL, PRD, PFIX). Pin fields to create per-environment templates; field names shown here can be used directly in template variable references.
      

      

        🔍 Step A — Build per-environment templates (one-time setup): Select an environment below, fetch a CHG from that environment, then 📌 pin the fields that should default for every future ticket in that environment. REL and PRD often differ in assignment group, implementation plan, etc. When done, copy a template to save time, then head to the Generator tab.
      

      

        ✅

📋 CTASK Templates

          
        

        

          
ServiceNow automatically creates 2 CTASKs when a CHG is submitted. The CRG will update those with the correct names and descriptions below. Add custom templates for any additional CTASKs that need to be created (e.g. Business Checkout — PRD only).


          
          

            
            

              
              
            

            
CTASK name will be: [prefix] - [ENV] e.g. "Enrollment - AWS - PRD"

        ▶ Advanced Options
      

      

        

          
🔑 Personal Access Token (PAT)

          
If your Jira instance supports PATs, you can bypass the relay bookmarklet entirely.
             Generate a PAT in Jira → Profile → Personal Access Tokens, then use it directly
             in API requests. This requires a local proxy server to avoid CORS restrictions.

          curl -H "Authorization: Bearer YOUR_PAT" https://jira.example.com/rest/api/2/myself
          
Toolbox doesn't natively support PAT mode yet — this is documented for
             power users who want to build their own proxy. A PowerShell proxy script example:

          $listener = [System.Net.HttpListener]::new()
$listener.Prefixes.Add("http://localhost:9222/")
$listener.Start()
# Forward requests to Jira with PAT header attached
        

        

          
🌐 PowerShell Proxy Server

          
Run a lightweight local proxy that adds authentication headers to every request,
             eliminating the need for the relay bookmarklet. The Toolbox would connect to
             localhost:9222
             instead of using postMessage.

          
This approach requires PowerShell and a running terminal window. It's more
             convenient for power users who already have a terminal open, but less accessible
             for general users.

        

        

          
🪟 Edge Launcher (--disable-web-security)

          
Launch a separate Edge profile with --disable-web-security
             to allow direct cross-origin Jira API calls. This eliminates the relay entirely
             but runs in a dedicated browser instance with relaxed security.

          msedge --user-data-dir="C:\temp\toolbox-edge" --disable-web-security --disable-site-isolation-trials "toolbox.html"
          
⚠️ Security note: This disables same-origin policy for ALL tabs
             in that browser instance. Only use a dedicated profile for Toolbox.

        

      


    


  


  

  
  


  

    

      
      
      
      
      
    


    
    


      

        🐛 PRB Generator — Generates a structured Problem Record (PRB) in ServiceNow from a Jira defect or story. Step 1: Fetch the source Jira issue  ·  Step 2: Map fields to SNow PRB format  ·  Step 3: Review and submit or copy. Field mappings are configurable in Settings.

            0 fields
            
            
            
            
            
          

        

        

          

            Configure which Jira fields to populate on every create. Preset = fixed value  |  From SNow = maps a ServiceNow PRB field  |  Auto-Format ✦ = template string using {token} (e.g. {incNumber}: {number}: {short_description})  |  You Fill In = prompted each time.

        ⏱ Work Log — Tracks time spent on Jira issues manually or via the built-in timer. The timer runs in the background — switching views does not stop it.
        📅 Today: all log entries for the current calendar day  · 
        📜 History: past 30 days of entries, grouped by day  · 
        Export generates a CSV of all logged time entries.

✏ Manual Jira Comment Poster

          

            Paste a branch name, commit SHA, or commit message. ⚙ Git Hook Generator

          

            Generate PowerShell git hooks that automatically post a Jira comment after every commit or merge — no browser needed.
            Requires a Jira PAT (set it in the Settings tab above).
          


          

            Installation steps:

            1. Download the scripts below

            2. Run install-hooks.ps1 -RepoPath "C:\path\to\your\repo"

               Or for global hooks: install-hooks.ps1 -Global

            3. Commit anything — Toolbox will post to Jira automatically

    |
    📊 Team Dashboard
    
    
    
    
  

  

    Jira
    
    
    
  

  


    


    


    

      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
      
    


    

      

        📊 Overview — Active sprint (Scrum) or current in-progress work (Kanban) for this team. Health score is derived from blocked issues, stale work, and sprint pace.
        🟢 On Track: no concerns  · 
        🟡 Watch: 1–2 concerns  · 
        🔴 At Risk: 3+ blocked or behind pace.
      

      
Enter your Jira project key in ⚙ Settings to load your sprint.

    


    

      

        👥 By Assignee — Groups all active sprint issues by assignee. Shows each person's in-progress, to-do, and done counts. Unassigned work is grouped separately at the bottom.
      

      
Load a sprint first.

    


    

      

        🚫 Blockers — Three categories of at-risk work, sorted by days since last update (oldest first).
        Blocked: status = Blocked/On Hold/Impeded or has "is blocked by" link  · 
        Stale: In Progress/Dev/Review for 5+ days with no update  · 
        Flagged: Jira Impediment marker set.
      

      
Load a sprint first.

    


    

      

        🐛 Defect Management — Full bug radar for the dev lead.
        Pulls the last 90 days of bugs from this project (not just the active sprint) so nothing slips through.
        ⚠️ Needs Triage: open bugs with no assignee, sorted by priority & age  · 
        🏃 In Progress: who is fixing what right now  · 
        ⚠️ Stale: in-progress bug not updated in 3+ days.
        Priority matrix shows where your backlog is concentrated. Assignee workload bars show who is carrying the most bugs.
      

      
Load a sprint first.

    


    

      

        

          🗣️ Standup — Run your daily standup right here.
          🏃 Board Walk sweeps columns right-to-left (Done → In Progress → To Do) — the Kanban-native format that surfaces oldest stuck work first.
          Cards are age-coded: ● Green = fresh (≤2 days) · ● Yellow = aging (3–5 days) · ● Red = overdue (6+ days).
          Expand any card to post an update comment or raise a blocker directly to Jira.
          👤 Person Walk iterates by assignee — better for smaller Scrum teams.
          Use the ⏱️ 15-min timer to keep standups crisp.
        

        

          

            ⏱ Timer
            15:00
            
            
          

          

            
            
            
          

        

        

      

    


    

      

        

          📅 Planning — Scope and prioritise upcoming work without leaving Toolbox.
          Select a Release (fixVersion) to focus the view, then group by 📅 Release → 🗂️ Feature (Epic) → 👤 Assignee to see who is delivering what and how much capacity remains.
          Progress bars on each Epic section show done/in-progress/todo counts. Issues not assigned to a release appear under — Unscheduled —.
          Switch grouping at any time using the buttons in the toolbar — the data reflows instantly without a new Jira fetch.
        

        

          
          
          

          

            
            
            
          

        

        

      

    


    

      

        🎯 Pointing — Story pointing queue: issues without story points (null or 0), ordered by priority (Highest → High → Medium → Low → None), then by creation date (oldest first). Use 🏠 → arrows to navigate, Skip to defer, or select a point value to commit.
      

      

      
Load a sprint first.

    


    

      

        
Team Dashboard Settings

        

          
          
        

        

          
          

            
            
          

          
Leave blank and click Search to list all boards you have access to (both Scrum & Kanban).

        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
          
Default: 1,2,3,5,8,13,21 — adjust to match exactly what your Jira story points dropdown offers

        

        

          
          
          
If your Jira uses a dropdown for story points (e.g. "Story Points Value"), enter its field ID here. Use the Field Picker tool to find it.

        

        

          
          
          
The field that links issues to their parent Feature or Epic. Auto-detected from fields named "Feature Link" or "Epic Link". Use the Field Picker tool to find the exact ID if needed.

        

        
      

      

        
📈 Metrics Configuration

        

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
          

          

            
            
            
Leave blank to auto-detect using Jira's status category (recommended). Only fill in if your workflow requires matching specific status names.

          

          

          

            
            
            
Enter your team's starting cycle time to track progress toward 20% reduction goal

          

          

            
            
          

        

      

    


    

      

        📈 Metrics — Sprint velocity (avg story points completed per sprint)  ·  Predictability (% of committed stories completed without mid-sprint additions, 🎯 80% target)  ·  Cycle time (In Progress → Done, calendar days)  ·  Throughput (issues resolved per period). All metrics use closed sprint history from the Jira Agile API.
      

      

        

          
Performance Metrics

          
2026 Delivery Excellence · detecting board type...

        

        
      

      

      

        

          
Predictability 

          
Sprint committed vs completed · 🎯 80% target

        

        

          
Click "Load Metrics" to calculate predictability.

        

      

      

        

          
Cycle Time 

          
In Progress → Done · calendar days

        

        

          
Click "Load Metrics" to calculate cycle time.

        

      

      

        

          
Bottleneck Analysis

          
Average time spent in each workflow status · identify where work gets stuck

        

        

          
Click "Load Metrics" to analyze workflow bottlenecks.

        

      

      

        

          
Throughput 

          
Work items completed per period

        

        

          
Click "Load Metrics" to calculate throughput.

        

      

    


    

      

        📦 Releases — Fix versions with open issues for this team's project.
        🔴 Overdue: past release date with unresolved issues  · 
        🚨 Critical: ≤ 2 weeks  · 
        🟡 Watch: ≤ 4 weeks  · 
        🟢 On Track: > 4 weeks  · 
        🔔 No Date: unscheduled.
      

      

        

          
📦 Release Radar

          
Unreleased fix versions for this project

        

        
      

      

        

          
📦

          
No project key configured

          
Add a Project Key in ⚙ Settings to see the Release Radar.

        

      

    


    

      

        

          🔀 Pipeline
          

            
            
          

        

        
Every active REL story across the full testing pipeline. The REL story is the spine — it stays open until production deploy.
        ⚡ Day 4: SM decision point (extend or authorise REL deploy)  · 
        🔴 Day 7: hard deadline  · 
        INT window clock starts when INT env deployed is checked on the REL story checklist.
        Configure your Smart Checklist field ID in .

      

      

    


  







  

    
    |
    🚂 ART View
    
    
    
    
  

  

    Jira
    Confluence
    
    
    
    
  

  


    


    
    


    
    

      📅 PI:
      
      
      
      All data is scoped to the selected PI
    


    
    

      
🚂

      
Welcome to ART View

      
Add your teams in Settings to get started. Each team needs a Jira board ID.

      
    


    
    

      View as:
      
      
      
      
    

    


    
    

      
      
      
      
      
      
      
    


    
    


      
      

        

          

            📊
            ART Health Report
            — copy & paste into Slack, Teams, or email
            
          

          

            
          

          

            
            
          

        

      

      

        🚦 Overview — One card per team. Health is scored from blocked issues, stale work (no update in 5+ days), and sprint pacing. When a PI is selected above, all data is scoped to that PI across all teams.
        🟢 Green: on track  · 
        🟡 Yellow: 1–2 concerns  · 
        🔴 Red: 3+ blocked, 4+ stale, or significantly behind pace.
      

      

        Loading…
      

      

      

      

    


    
    

      

    


    
    

      

        📈 Predictability — The % of sprint-committed stories that were completed without being added mid-sprint. SAFe target: ≥ 80%.
        Scrum teams use sprint report history (last N sprints). Kanban teams use released fix versions and PI completion rate as a proxy.
        Below 80% consistently signals scope churn, unclear acceptance criteria, or capacity mismatches — all organizational impediments.
      

      

      

    


    
    

      

        📅 Releases — Fix versions with open issues across all ART teams.
        🔴 Overdue: past release date with unresolved issues  · 
        🟠 Critical: release ≤ 2 weeks away with open issues  · 
        🟡 Watch: ≤ 4 weeks  · 
        🟢 On Track: > 4 weeks or fully resolved  · 
        ⬜ No Date: unscheduled.
      

      

    


    
    
    

      

        🤝 Scrum of Scrums — One card per team. Auto-populated from coaching analysis. Click any card to edit responses. Edited fields are preserved on refresh unless explicitly cleared.
      

      

      

    


    
    


    
    

    
    

      

        🤝
        SoS — Team
        
      

      

        

      

      

        

        
        
      

    


    

      


        

          
Teams

          

            

              
                

                  Team Name
                  Board ID
                  Project Key
                  
                

              
              
            

            

              
              
              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            
Search returns board IDs. Enter the ID in the Board ID field above.

            
💡 SoS Issue Key: any Jira issue your team has access to (e.g. PROJ-123). SoS card data is stored on it so all toolbox instances share the same responses.

          

        


        

          
PI Configuration

          

            

              ℹ️ Two separate fields: The PI Field identifies which PI an issue belongs to. The Story Points Field stores the point estimate. They are different custom fields.
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              

                
                
              

              
            

            

              
              
            

            
            

              
              
            

            

              

                
                
              

              

                
                
              

            

            
Dates are extracted from the PI name (e.g. "Apr 13 - Jun 26, 2026"). You can also enter them manually.

          

        


        

          
Display Options

          

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

            

              
              
            

          

        


        

          
Monthly Report Settings

          

            

              
              
              
The ↑ Connect button on the Monthly Report tab will open this URL and prompt you to click the Toolbox Relay bookmarklet.

            

            

              
              

                
                
              

              
              
Default tries: customfield_10014, customfield_10008, customfield_10006. Set this if your Feature Link uses a different field.

            

            

              
              
            

            
P-Code default: clarityid. Update if the P-Code moves to a different Jira field.

        🔗 ServiceNow Integration (optional)
        ▼
      
      

        
        
        Enables clickable INC/PRB links in the Current Release section
      

    


    
    

      
        🧺 Quality / Dirty Laundry Settings (optional — Defect field IDs)
        ▼
      
      

        
Configure custom field IDs to enable the "Missing Root Cause" and "Missing Environment" dirty-laundry checks (Defects only).

        

          
          
        

        

          
          
        

      

    


    
    


    
    

      

        
📋

        
Enter your project key above to get started

        
Example: ENFCT

      

    

  








  

    
    |
    ⚙️ Toolbox Settings
    
  


  


    
    

      

        
Jira Connection

        
      

      

        

          
          
        

        
        

          
          
        

        
        

          

            A PAT lets Toolbox call Jira directly. Generate one at: Jira Profile → Security → Personal Access Tokens.

            ⚠ Stored in browser localStorage — do not use on shared computers.
          

          

            

              
              
            

          

          

            
            
            
          

        

        
        

          

            

              1
              

                One-time setup: Drag this link to your bookmarks bar

                

                  📎 Toolbox Relay
                  ← Drag to bookmarks bar — works on Jira and ServiceNow
                

              

            

            

              2
              

                Open Jira: Opens (or focuses) a Jira tab using your saved URL
                

                  
                

              

            

            

              3
              

                Activate relay: Click the 📎 Toolbox Relay bookmark on the Jira tab
              

            

          

          

            
          

          

        

      

    


    
    

      
Advanced Jira Fields

      

        

          
          
          
Required only for Pipeline View write operations (recording Day 4 decisions by marking checklist items). Reading the checklist state for the INT window clock works automatically without this ID. Your Jira admin can find it at Jira Settings → Issues → Custom Fields.

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
ServiceNow Connection

        
      

      

        

          
          
        

        

          Use the same 📎 Toolbox Relay bookmarklet from the Jira section — it auto-detects which site you're on.
        

        

          

            1
            

              Open ServiceNow: Opens (or focuses) a SNow tab using your saved URL
              

                
              

            

          

          

            2
            

              Activate relay: Click the 📎 Toolbox Relay bookmark on the ServiceNow tab — it auto-detects ServiceNow.
            

          

        

        

          
        

        

      

    


    
    

      

        
Confluence Connection

        
      

      

        

          
          
        

        

          Required for ART View Monthly Report auto-fill with Rovo. Use the same 📎 Toolbox Relay bookmarklet — it auto-detects Confluence.
        

        

          

            1
            

              Open Confluence: Opens (or focuses) a Confluence tab using your saved URL
              

                
              

            

          

          

            2
            

              Activate relay: Click the 📎 Toolbox Relay bookmark on the Confluence tab — it auto-detects Confluence.
            

          

        

        

          
        

        

      

    


    
    

      
📎 Confluence Sync

      

        

          Keep toolbox.html hosted centrally on a Confluence page. Drag this bookmarklet to your browser toolbar.
          When you want to publish a new release, open your target Confluence page (SSO logged in) and click it —
          it fetches the latest toolbox.html from GitHub Releases and uploads it as an attachment.
        

        

          📎 Sync to Confluence
          ← Drag to bookmarks bar, then click on your Confluence page
        

        

          💡 Works with Okta/Azure SSO — no API token needed. The bookmarklet uses your browser session.
        

      

    


    
    

      
🎨 Theme & Display

      

        
        
      

    


    
    

      
🧙 Setup Wizard

      

        Walk through the interactive connection setup again at any time.
        
      

    


  









  

    
    |
    🤖 AI Chat
    Local LLM · Ollama · llamafile · No internet required
    
  


  


    
    

      
      
    


    
    

      
      

        
        Not connected — configure endpoint in Settings
        
        
      


      
      


      
      

        
        
      

    


    
    

      

        
🔌 Endpoint

        

          

            
            
            

              Ollama: http://localhost:11434/v1
               · 
              llamafile: http://localhost:8080/v1
            

          

          

            
            
            

              Examples: qwen2.5-coder:7b
               ·  llama3.2:3b
               ·  phi4-mini
               ·  gemma3:4b
            

          

          
        

      


      

        
🧠 System Prompt

        

          
          
Prepended to every conversation. Applies on next message after saving.

          
        

      


      

        
ℹ️ Quick Start

        

          

            Ollama — Installs to your user profile (no admin needed on most corporate setups).

            Download from ollama.com, then pull a model:

            ollama pull qwen2.5-coder:7b
          

          

            llamafile — Single .exe, no install required. Just download and run.

            Get one from github.com/Mozilla-Ocho/llamafile and double-click it.

            Then set endpoint to http://localhost:8080/v1
          

          

            🔒 All traffic stays on localhost — nothing leaves your machine.
